summary_one_line: A SQL expression whose exprDup copy path hits the line-100843 true branch, reached only by token-rich operator expressions not plain digit lists.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SELECT input parsing into an expression tree whose exprDup duplication exercises the structural sub-case gated at sqlite3.c:100843 — produced by short token-dense operator expressions (e.g. 'SELECT' followed by runs of '+'/'-' and an '@'-prefixed token) rather than long monotone comma-separated digit lists.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds begin with 'SELECT' and contain dense runs of operator bytes (e.g. '-+++++++' and '@d') and reach the true side of the blocker (W T=2 F=66 at L100843), where cmplog's CMP-buffer-driven substitution let it land the operator/token bytes that build the gating expression structure.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking naive seeds are long comma-separated digit runs ('SELECT   111111,111111111,111,...') and never take the true side (L T=0 F=285 at L100843); their bytes are concentrated on '1'(0x31)/','(0x2c) digit-list dataflow, not the operator/token bytes the winning side needs.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of naive's havoc/token stack: it reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input, letting it place the exact token bytes that drive exprDup down the line-100843 true path. naive lacks any CMP introspection so it only stumbles on digit-list mutations and never constructs the gating expression.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of sqlite3.c:100843 inside exprDup, globally blocked.
cmplog beats naive decisively: 8/10 resolve vs 0/10, the only decisive pair, attributed to I2S.
The loser never takes the true side of the blocker while the winner sometimes does.
Winner-resolving seeds are SELECT inputs with dense operator/token runs including '+' and '@d'.
Loser-blocking naive seeds are long monotone comma-separated digit lists.
At the divergent offsets the winner carries operator/token bytes while the loser is concentrated on '1' and ',' digit-list bytes.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S — only the havoc/token stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 14549 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed f9361da7631b2ce7`; the chain has 19 ancestor levels and the tool reported 'I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 17, 18' (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only), the lower-bound I2S signal exclusive to cmplog/vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic SELECT harness where naive (no CMP introspection) takes the line-100843 true side at the same rate as cmplog within budget, showing the operator/token expression structure is reachable by plain havoc/digit mutation without I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: Without on-disk source for line 100843 the precise expression sub-case the true branch gates is inferred from the W/L byte structure and exprDup context rather than read directly.
